description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese katana sword, featuring a long, gently curved, single-edged steel blade with a sharp point. The blade is polished and reflective, with a visible hamon line running along its edge. The handle is wrapped in a dark material, likely ray skin and cord, and is capped with a gold-colored pommel. A circular hand guard (tsuba) separates the blade from the handle, also finished in a gold tone. The sword comes with a matching black lacquered wooden sheath (saya) adorned with gold accents and a red-brown cord wrap. The overall craftsmanship and detailing suggest a high-quality, decorative, or ceremonial piece.
description: A traditional Japanese katana with a curved steel blade, gold-accented hand guard, and a black lacquered sheath with red-brown cord wrap.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the katana blade and its sheath, highlighting the curve, gold accents, and cord-wrapped handle.
description_view_1: End-on view showing the narrow profile of the blade and sheath, with the circular guard and pommel visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view of the katana and sheath, displaying the blade's curvature and the decorative elements on the sheath.
description_view_3: Direct top-down view showing the alignment of the blade and sheath, with the hand guard and pommel clearly visible.
attribute_handle_design: wrapped, cylindrical handle with gold pommel and red-brown cord
attribute_ornamentation: gold accents on guard, pommel, and sheath; red-brown cord wrap
